prompt: I'm studying whether a job training program affects earnings. I believe the training works through two channels: (1) directly improving skills, and (2) indirectly by increasing confidence, which leads to better job interviews. I want to decompose the total effect into the direct effect (skills) and indirect effect (confidence → interviews → earnings). I have data on 1000 participants: training (binary), confidence_score (continuous), interview_performance (continuous), and annual_earnings (continuous). Build me a structural causal model to estimate the direct and indirect effects.
assertions: Proposes a DAG with: training → confidence → interview_performance → earnings AND training → earnings (direct path)
Asks the user to confirm the DAG, especially whether there are unmeasured confounders between confidence and earnings
Identifies this as a mediation analysis requiring a structural causal model (not a quasi-experiment)
Uses pm.do()/pm.observe() or Bayesian SEM with PyMC to estimate interventional distributions or structural effects (not frequentist regression)
Distinguishes between Natural Direct Effect (NDE) and Natural Indirect Effect (NIE)
States the assumptions required for mediation (no unmeasured mediator-outcome confounding, no treatment-induced confounding)
Asks the user whether these mediation assumptions are defensible
Reports both direct and indirect effects with uncertainty (credible intervals)
Warns that mediation analysis requires stronger assumptions than total effect estimation
Produces or offers to produce a report with the DAG, assumptions, effects decomposition, and limitations
